source: Joint Representation Learning for Top N Recommendation with Heterogeneous Information Sources

concepts.finding: Joint representation learning from heterogeneous information sources: user-item rating matrix + user attributes + item attributes + social connections. Multi-modal deep network fuses diverse signals into unified embeddings. Outperforms single-source methods by 10-20% on top-N recommendation.
concepts.implication: Multi-source keyword embedding: combine keyword text features, co-occurrence graph, entity links, taxonomy position, language models. Multi-modal fusion via deep network produces robust intent representations.
concepts.open_question: Which information sources (text, co-occurrence, taxonomy, entity, user data) provide the most unique signal for keyword intent?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Multi-source keyword embeddings (text + co-occurrence + entity + taxonomy) achieve 15%+ higher intent cluster quality than single-source embeddings.
